questions.question: Explain the purpose of try, catch, and throw keywords in C++ exception handling with an example.
keyPoints: try block encloses code that might throw an exception
catch block handles the thrown exception
throw keyword is used to raise an exception
Syntax: try { throw value; } catch(type e) { // handle }

questions.question: What is stack unwinding in C++? Explain with an example scenario.
keyPoints: Stack unwinding is the process of destroying local objects as exception propagates
Destructors are called for each local object during unwinding
Ensures proper resource cleanup even when exceptions occur
Example: function A calls B, B calls C; if C throws, destructors of C's locals run, then B's, then control reaches A's catch

questions.question: Write a C++ program demonstrating creation and usage of custom exception class.
keyPoints: Custom exception should derive from std::exception
Override what() method to return descriptive message
Use throw to throw custom exception object
Catch by reference to polymorphic type

questions.question: Explain the three levels of exception safety guarantees with examples.
keyPoints: No-throw guarantee: function never throws, always succeeds
Strong guarantee: operation either completes or has no effect
Basic guarantee: object remains in valid state (may be changed)
Example: std::vector::push_back provides strong guarantee

questions.question: What is the purpose of catch(...) block? When and why should it be used?
keyPoints: catch(...) catches all types of exceptions
Useful for cleanup operations before rethrowing
Should not swallow exceptions without proper handling
Often used as last catch block for logging or cleanup

questions.question: Explain the concept of rethrowing an exception with throw; statement.
keyPoints: throw; without operand rethrows current exception
Used when catch block can partially handle exception
Passes exception to outer try-catch block
Useful for logging before propagating

questions.question: What is the noexcept specifier? Explain its usage and importance.
keyPoints: noexcept indicates function won't throw exceptions
If noexcept function throws, std::terminate() is called
Enables compiler optimizations
Destructors should be noexcept by default

questions.question: Write a complete C++ program demonstrating exception handling with multiple catch blocks, custom exception, and rethrowing. Include proper exception safety considerations.
keyPoints: Define custom exception class deriving from std::exception
Use try-catch with multiple catch blocks for different types
Demonstrate catch(...) for general handling
Show rethrowing with throw; statement
Include proper destructors demonstrating stack unwinding
Handle both standard and custom exceptions
Ensure basic/strong exception safety guarantees

questions.question: Explain the standard exception class hierarchy in C++. Create a program that demonstrates catching different standard exceptions.
keyPoints: std::exception is base class
Two main branches: std::logic_error and std::runtime_error
logic_error: invalid_argument, length_error, out_of_range
runtime_error: overflow_error, underflow_error, out_of_range
Demonstrate catching in order from most specific to general
Use what() method to get error messages

questions.question: Explain the concept of exception safety in C++. Discuss different guarantee levels and demonstrate with examples.
keyPoints: Exception safety ensures program remains in valid state
Four levels: no-throw, strong, basic, no guarantee
No-throw: operations never throw (destructors)
Strong: either succeeds or no change (std::vector::insert)
Basic: valid but possibly changed state
Provide code examples demonstrating each level
Discuss RAII and its role in exception safety

questions.question: Write a C++ program using function try blocks in constructors to handle exceptions from member initialization list.
keyPoints: Function try blocks associate catch with entire function body
Useful for catching exceptions from base class and member initialization
Must rethrow if catch block cannot fully handle
Demonstrate with base class and member objects that may throw
